description:
As APISYS
  I want to call the merchant cancel wager API
  So that I can refund a previously paid wager

a prepared paid wager fixture exists for:
the AMO008 response should be successful
the wallet balance in "<currency>" should increase by 30

name: AMO010 Request Transfer In

description:
As APISYS
  I want to call the merchant transfer out API
  So that I can debit funds from the member wallet

steps.name: the member has positive wallet balance in "<currency>"
I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>"
the AMO011 response should be successful
the wallet balance in "<currency>" should decrease by 20.5

elements.name: Repeating the same transfer_no is idempotent

steps.name: the member has positive wallet balance in "<currency>"
I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>"
the AMO011 response should be successful
I store the response field "reference_id" as "amo011_reference_id"
the wallet balance in "<currency>" should decrease by 15
I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>"
the AMO011 response should be successful
the wallet balance in "<currency>" should remain unchanged

elements.name: Insufficient balance returns failed status

steps.name: I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>"
I prepare an amount exceeding the balance by 10
the AMO011 response should be successful
the wallet balance in "<currency>" should remain unchanged

elements.name: Not providing amount transfers out all integer wallet balance

steps.name: the member has positive wallet balance in "<currency>"
I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>"
the AMO011 response should be successful
the response amount should equal the integer part of the recorded wallet balance in "<currency>"
the wallet balance in "<currency>" should equal the remaining decimal balance

name: AMO011 Request Transfer Out

description:
As APISYS
  I want to call the merchant cancel transfer API
  So that I can reverse a previous transfer ledger

elements.name: Cancel transfer returns reference_id for an existing transfer in and is idempotent

steps.name: a successful transfer in exists for:
the AMO014 response should be successful
I store the response field "reference_id" as "cancel_reference_id"
the AMO014 response should be successful

elements.name: Cancel transfer returns reference_id for an existing transfer out and is idempotent

steps.name: the member has positive wallet balance in "<currency>"
a successful transfer out exists for:
the AMO014 response should be successful
I store the response field "reference_id" as "cancel_reference_id"
the AMO014 response should be successful

elements.name: Cancel transfer returns reference_id when transfer does not exist and is idempotent

steps.name: the AMO014 response should be successful
I store the response field "reference_id" as "cancel_reference_id"
the AMO014 response should be successful

name: AMO014 Request Cancel Transfer

description:
As APISYS
  I want to cancel an actual existing transfer
  So that repeated cancel calls behave consistently

elements.name: Transfer in then cancel twice returns the same reference_id

steps.name: I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>"
the AMO010 response should be successful
the AMO014 response should be successful
I store the response field "reference_id" as "cancel_reference_id_1"
the AMO014 response should be successful
the wallet balance in "<currency>" should remain unchanged

name: AMO014 Cancel transfer integration flow

description:
As APISYS
  I want to call the merchant transfer APIs in sequence
  So that I can verify full integer-balance withdrawal behavior end to end

elements.name: Transfer in 120.5 then transfer out without amount deducts 120 and leaves 0.5

steps.name: a merchant member exists with platform username "<platform_username>"
the wallet balance for "<platform_username>" in "<currency>" should become 120.5
the wallet balance for "<platform_username>" in "<currency>" should become 0.5
APISYS requests member wallet balances with:

name: Integration Flow - Transfer out all integer balance

As APISYS
  I want to call the merchant wallet APIs in sequence
  So that I can verify transfer credit, debit, reversal, and balance retrieval end to end

steps.name: a merchant member exists with platform username "<platform_username>"
the wallet balance for "<platform_username>" in "<currency>" should become 100
APISYS requests member wallet balances with:

APISYS requests member wallet balances with:
the wallet balance for "<platform_username>" in "<currency>" should become 100
